mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window or positive return frequency drops below 50% during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 5.0 percent, labor market slack signals weakening consumer demand and corporate earnings deterioration, driving negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 5-day returns in more than 55 percent of instances where UNRATE surpasses 5.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates synchronized domestic demand growth, which sustains corporate revenue expansion and produces positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to generate a statistically significant positive return differential over 60-day horizons.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during periods of stable inflation reflects robust manufacturing capacity utilization and supply-side momentum, which directly translates to positive S&P 500 forward returns over one month.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration yields negative or flat 20-day returns when CPIAUCSL month-over-month growth exceeds 0.4 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid consumer price inflation triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, which compresses equity valuations and forces negative S&P 500 returns within the following trading week.
failure_condition: Equity markets record positive 5-day returns in over 60 percent of months where CPIAUCSL rises faster than 0.3 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Rising nonfarm payrolls combined with a declining unemployment rate confirms a tightening labor market that supports wage growth and consumer spending, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The combined signal of PAYEMS growth and UNRATE decline produces a negative rank IC over any 24-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real gross domestic product reflects broad economic expansion that lifts corporate earnings expectations, producing consistently positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Positive GDPC1 growth correlates with negative 20-day equity returns in more than half of observed quarters.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle signals supply-side contraction that outweighs demand resilience, triggering negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during tightening cycles fail to predict negative 5-day returns, with positive return frequency exceeding 55 percent.
